title: Assistant Superintendent / Director of Preschool, Special Education & Student Services
notes: Buckeye Local SD in Jefferson County (Dillonvale/Rayland), Coy Sudvary is Superintendent (csudvary@omeresa.net). Julie Packer was named Assistant Superintendent in March 2024, with explicit oversight of preschool, special education, federal programs, and 21st Century Grant — direct ECE fit. Was Buckeye South Elementary Principal (2022 Jefferson County Principal of the Year). District opened new autism preschool unit at Buckeye North in 2023. Email inferred from confirmed district domain buckeyelocal.net pattern (ZoomInfo confirms j***@buckeyelocal.net).

title: Superintendent
notes: Small district (~1,100 students PK-12, 4 schools) with no dedicated district-level curriculum or ECE director. Superintendent Matthew Bowen is the appropriate central-office target. District implementing Science of Reading-aligned curriculum and professional learning; Ohio DEW has flagged district for expansion efforts. Met with ODEW in 2024-25.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small PK-12 district (~900 students) in Wyandot County. No dedicated district-level curriculum or ECE director surfaced in staff directory; Mr. Wank is the central-office decision-maker. District operates preschool through Carey Elementary. Note: district email domain is careyevs.org (not careyschools.org).

title: Elementary Principal (K-6) - oversees curriculum and ECE
notes: Small district (~1,000 students K-12, 3 schools). Superintendent Ryan Gallwitz (ryan.gallwitz@centerburgschools.org) is the central admin; no separate curriculum director. Miguel Thompson is K-6 elementary principal with expertise in curriculum development and is the closest ECE/elementary lead. Use Gallwitz as alternative.

title: Assistant Superintendent / Curriculum & Instruction Coordinator (Special Education)
notes: Lawrence County district. Jamie Shields holds dual role as Assistant Superintendent / Director of Curriculum and Special Education. District received $10.3M federal allocation in 2024 to expand programs including ECE — strong buying signal. Marshall University and Ohio State graduate.

title: Butler Elementary Principal / District Curriculum Director
notes: Richland County district (Bellville). Libby Nickoli holds dual role as Butler Elementary Principal and District Curriculum Director (also previously special education coordinator). District was in superintendent search in early 2025 (13 applicants). Email format inferred from district's clearfork.k12.oh.us domain — confidence medium as not directly verified on public site.

title: Superintendent
notes: Mercer County district. Doug Mader hired as superintendent effective Aug 2022 with explicit curriculum and instruction expertise. Small district with no separate central curriculum director — Mader is the appropriate decision-maker. District operates the Cavalier Preschool program. Note: district has migrated to coldwatercavs.org.

title: EMIS Coordinator / Administrative Assistant
notes: K-12 STEAM charter / independent public school in Greene County (~155 students grades 6-10 in available data; growing to K-12). No published superintendent or curriculum director name in search results. Amanda Crabtree (EMIS Coordinator) had prior special-needs preschool paraprofessional experience but is administrative support, not a curriculum decision-maker. Main office number is best route — Email needed via Apollo.io.

title: PreK-6 Principal
notes: Small rural Hancock County K-12 district (~600 students). No dedicated curriculum director; superintendent Heath Huffman (huffman@cory-rawson.org) is the alt contact. Ben Thiel as PreK-6 Principal is the best ECE-adjacent contact. District uses Eureka Math K-6, LLI and SIPPS literacy interventions. Title I expanded to K-6.

title: Superintendent
notes: Columbiana County district. Small district (~1,000 students). No separate curriculum director on directory; Hill is the central decision-maker. District building a new K-12 campus (occupancy permit Sept 2025 for athletic center; campus opening 2026) — major capital project with curriculum review opportunity. District uses crestviewlocal.k12.oh.us for official email.

title: Superintendent
notes: Larger Summit County district. Dr. Andrea Celico became Superintendent Aug 2022. Prior Director of Curriculum (Dr. Amy Cruse) left for Parma City Schools in July 2023 — position appears to be in transition. Julie Dudones (6-12) and Dr. Rachael Seifert (K-5) serve as Coordinators of Curriculum and Instruction (rseifert@cfalls.org would be best K-5/ECE contact if directly verified). District's preschool program earned Gold rating under Ohio Step Up To Quality (SUTQ) — strong ECE program. Email format confirmed as first-initial+lastname@cfalls.org.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Lawrence County K-12 district (Coal Grove). No separate curriculum director. Dean Mader serves as Director of Federal Programs (dean.mader@db.k12.oh.us) and may also be relevant for Title I-funded ECE. District has Title I Parent program at elementary level.

title: Curriculum Coordinator
notes: Tuscarawas County district. Tennille Williams is the Curriculum Coordinator; Assistant Superintendent is Seth Corder. Superintendent is Karie McCrate. District uses dovertornadoes.com domain. K-5 elementary handbook references ECE/early literacy programming.

title: Director of Curriculum and School Improvement
notes: Columbiana County district. Ryan Willis was promoted to Director of Curriculum and School Improvement at July 2023 BOE meeting (previously Associate Principal at the HS). Superintendent is Jonathan Ludwig. District actively working on state report card grade improvement and attendance initiatives — strong reason for curriculum outreach. District now uses elpotters.school as alt domain but elcsd.k12.oh.us is the established official email domain.

title: Superintendent
notes: Eastern Local in Meigs County (Reedsville/Beaver/Tuppers Plains) — Ohio Purple Star district, ~734 students PK-12. Nick Dettwiller named superintendent in 2024. Small district with no dedicated curriculum director — Dettwiller is the appropriate contact. District has had two failed levies in 2024-25 (budget pressure context for outreach). NOTE: input lists ep.k12.oh.us but current district uses easternlocal.net / easternlocal.com domains.

title: Curriculum Director
notes: Erie/Huron County district (~1,500 students PK-12, Milan/Berlin Heights). Dean Stanfield holds the Curriculum Director role. District serves PreK through preschool program. Email pattern inferred from edisonchargers.org domain — confidence supported by district directory page.

title: Superintendent (former Chief Academic Officer)
notes: Union County district (Milford Center). Mrs. Janel Chapman became Superintendent in 2025; previously served as Chief Academic Officer and HS Principal — strong curriculum/academic background. District has a dedicated Teaching & Learning page. She championed authentic learning convocation for ~180 staff Aug 2025. Education in music ed (Miami), counseling (UD), and admin (OSU).

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Lake County PK-12 district. Dr. Karl Schneider named new superintendent effective May 1, 2026 (succeeding William Billington). No dedicated curriculum director surfaced. Email format inferred from district pattern (Billington was wbillington@fhevs.org, dpaolo@fhevs.org confirmed for prior superintendent). District does personalized learning initiatives — historic Hooked on Education partnership with Mentor Schools.

title: Superintendent
notes: Clermont County PK-12 district. Tim Hart serves as Director of Curriculum, Instruction & Assessment but his current email is hart_t@ccesc.org (Clermont County ESC) indicating curriculum services routed through the ESC. Gibson as Superintendent is the direct district decision-maker. District domain for email is felicityohioschools.org (not felicityschools.org).

title: Superintendent
notes: Mercer County district (~1,000 students). Tony Stahl is current superintendent (previously HS Principal named 2020). Justin Firks (prior superintendent) departed for Bethel/Elida. Five-Star state report card rated district. Small district with no separate central curriculum director. Email format follows district pattern (e.g., firksj@fortrecoveryschools.org).

title: Superintendent (former Director of Curriculum, Assessment & Staff Development)
notes: Sandusky County district (~3,500 students). Denice Hirt is Superintendent; previously held district Director of Curriculum role. Anthony Walker is Assistant Director of College and Career Readiness in the Curriculum Department. District runs Integrated Preschool program for ages 3-5 (serves children with special needs alongside typically developing peers) — ECE fit. All-day kindergarten in all four elementaries. Note: ZoomInfo shows h***@fremont.k12.oh.us but district website uses fremontschools.net domain consistently.

title: Superintendent
notes: Ashtabula County PK-12 district (~2,000 students). Dr. Paul Lombardo is current Superintendent (contract Aug 2025-July 2028; replaced interim David Riley following Terri Hrina-Treharn departure). Previously assistant superintendent at Cleveland Heights-University Heights. No dedicated district curriculum director surfaced. Email format inferred from genevaschools.org domain — confidence medium. District has been navigating leadership transition and equity concerns in recent years.

title: Chief Academic Officer
notes: STEM/STEAM Designated Independent public school in Springfield serving grades 6-12 (note: NOT a traditional ECE district — grade band starts at 6). Josh Jennings is Founding Director/Superintendent (jjennings@gisaoh.org). Truebenbach as Chief Academic Officer is the curriculum decision-maker. District focuses on agriculture/biosciences/food sciences. Recently broke ground on $16.9M facility expansion. Lower-priority ECE fit given grade band but maintain CAO contact.

title: Superintendent
notes: Greene County district (Jamestown), ~1,250 students. Joe Parish is current Superintendent. No dedicated curriculum director surfaced. District migrated to greeneview.org domain. Email format inferred (first.last@greeneview.org) — confidence medium pending direct verification.

title: Superintendent (retiring July 31, 2025)
notes: Defiance County district (~865 students K-12, 3 schools). Keith Countryman retiring July 31; Aaron Hylander (current HS Principal) succeeds him effective Aug 1, 2025 (ahylander@hicksvilleschools.org). District received $5K grant for The Zone curriculum; reviewing Cross-Cat curriculum. Small district with no dedicated curriculum director.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Henry County PK-12 district (~393 students, single building). Jessica Schuette named Superintendent March 2023, formerly Crestview Elementary Principal. Strong elementary background (BS in elementary/special ed, MEd in brain-based teaching). Spencer Root is MS/HS Principal. Mrs. Ashley Ensign is current Elementary Principal — alt ECE contact.

title: Superintendent
notes: Ross County district (Chillicothe area). Pete Ruby is current Superintendent and has been driving district-wide planning communications. No separate district curriculum director identified. Dan Riddle serves as Director of Special Programs. Small rural district.

title: Superintendent (retiring after 2025-26)
notes: Tuscarawas County district (Gnadenhutten), ~1,657 students K-12. Dr. Ira Wentworth retiring after 2025-26 school year. Amy Wentworth (his wife, current New Philadelphia City Schools superintendent) hired Jan 2026 to succeed him effective Aug 1, 2026 ($145K, 3-year contract). Leadership transition is active outreach opportunity. District has Curriculum Department page.

title: Superintendent
notes: Montgomery County (Dayton-area) district. Ronda Welch hired as Superintendent effective March 9, 2026 (after Rusty Clifford served as interim through July 2025; predecessor Richard Gates was placed on leave April 2024 pending investigation). Welch previously a part-time expulsion hearing officer at Columbus City Schools. District is in active transition — strong outreach timing. Email format inferred from district domain.

title: Superintendent
notes: Among smallest school districts in Ohio — single school on Lake Erie island with only 5 students for 2024-25 (3 boys, 2 girls). Three core teachers plus specialists. Ben Ohlemacher (3rd school year as superintendent) commutes by ferry/plane. Not a viable ECE prospect at scale, but contact recorded for completeness.

title: Superintendent
notes: Lake County district. Dr. Chad VanArnhem is Superintendent; holds Master's in Curriculum and Instruction (Ashland) and was previously Kirtland Elementary Principal. He has championed wellness-as-instruction connections. No dedicated central curriculum director. Sandy Martz is Treasurer/Assistant to Superintendent.

title: Superintendent
notes: Columbiana County district. Gerald (Tony) DelBoccio hired May 2024 (3-year contract, $105K) effective Aug 1, 2024, replacing Dennis Dunham who moved to Columbiana County ESC. DelBoccio was previously cleared after East Palestine theft accusation was dismissed. Small district with no separate curriculum director. Andy Tommelleo served as interim before DelBoccio.

title: Superintendent
notes: Fairfield County district (Baltimore, OH). Dr. Michael Johnson is current Superintendent (succeeded Paul Mathews who retired in 2018). District is building a new middle school — active capital project. No separate central curriculum director surfaced. Email format inferred from libertyunion.org domain — staff directory requires JavaScript and could not be directly extracted.

title: Superintendent
notes: Columbiana County district. Mr. Joseph Siefke is Superintendent. Two-school PK-12 district with no dedicated central curriculum director. Keith Edenfield is David Anderson Jr/Sr HS Principal; Dan Kemats is at McKinley Elementary — McKinley principal is alt ECE contact. District received Ohio Auditor of State Award 2024. Note: Siefke email uses OMERESA regional services (Ohio Mid-Eastern Regional Education Service Agency) which serves multiple districts. This is the official superintendent email used by the district per their site.

title: Superintendent
notes: Mahoning County PK-12 district. Mrs. Christine Sawicki is current Superintendent. Tracie Parry K-12 Principal; Jeff Hammond K-12 Asst Principal. No dedicated curriculum director. Note district uses lowellvilleschool.org for email despite k12.oh.us in URL of input.

title: District Curriculum Director
notes: Madison Local in Butler County (Middletown area, NOT Richland County's Madison Local). Charity Smith is the District Curriculum Director. Jeff Staggs is Superintendent. Three-school PK-12 district. District operates Madison Elementary serving grades PreK-5 — direct ECE fit.

title: Director of Curriculum and Instructional Technology
notes: Summit County district (south of Akron / New Franklin), ~1,200 students PK-12. Rachel Willis holds the Director of Curriculum and Instructional Technology role (3-year contract). District uses manchester-panthers.org for staff email (not panthercountry.org). Strong direct ECE/curriculum fit.

title: Director of Teaching and Learning (Curriculum)
notes: Larger Union County district — 5,400+ students, 5 elementaries (K-4), intermediate, MS, HS, and STEM Early College HS. Chris Campbell appointed Director of Teaching and Learning (curriculum equivalent). District actively piloting AI tools and announcing staff training/public guardrails ahead of June 2025 policy vote — innovation-forward. Strong ECE prospect given size and elementary footprint. Email format inferred from public staff email page pattern.

title: Superintendent (former 7-12 Principal)
notes: Small Hancock/Wood County district. Jeremy Herr is current Superintendent (familiar face — was the district's HS principal). No dedicated curriculum director. 2025-26 curriculum guide published for HS. Note district domain is mccombschool.org (input lists mccomblocalschool.org which redirects).

title: Superintendent (incoming Aug 1, 2026)
notes: Miami County district (~1,379 students K-12) — Five-star state report card 4 years running. Dr. Joseph Hoelzle approved April 30, 2026 as next Superintendent effective Aug 1, 2026, succeeding Eric Hughes (who left for Northmont). District is in leadership transition. No separate curriculum director surfaced. Email format inferred from district pattern. Outreach can target both Hoelzle and current acting/outgoing leadership.
